question_html:
Read the passage carefully and then answer the following questions: 10

We spend money for different reasons. We buy food, clothes or everyday essentials, pay for different services, entertain people, travel to places, help others in need or invest in business and thus spend money every day. In fact, spending is a part of our life.

Spending may make us happy or unhappy depending on how and why we spend. When we spend money on things that we need and within our limit, it is good. When it becomes a compulsive behaviour, it makes life stressful. Unnecessary spending or spending beyond one's means has some bad effects. For one thing, it may lead to financial ruin or debt, and for another, it may create unhappiness within families. People who overspend are never satisfied with what they have. They always rush for brands, fashion items, designer clothes etc. Over a period of time it becomes an addiction which may eventually create psychological problems.

Nowadays consumer items are displayed in stores or in advertisements in ways that they create a feeling of immediate need for them. We are constantly tempted to buy, use or consume things even when we do not have a genuine need. We all need to be careful here. Salespersons often encourage customers to buy things by flattering them. "This is a perfect match for you, they would say, or "You look so stunning in that dress." Never forget, they say the same thing to most of their customers. It is better not to be persuaded by such words. They use these words to please the customers as the more a customer buys, the higher the profit is. Overspending is not only related to shopping; it applies to other activities as well such as eating out. Many people buy too many items to eat in a restaurant. They can eat only some of them and the rest are wasted. It's not a responsible attitude. We cannot simply waste food because we have money to buy it.

Young people in a shopping mall often look at an item on display and think 'Oh I must buy this. I really need this.' They may not have the money needed in their wallet. So they use credit cards but using them is like taking a loan. If they are not careful, the loans increase which might lead them to a debt-trap.

Sometimes children insist on buying things that their parents cannot pay without stretching their budget. This may happen because the children's friends also have them. It's not fair as it becomes a burden for the parents.

Finally, don't get trapped by the glossy advertisements on television or the Internet. You should rather ask yourself: "Do I need this?" The best way to control the habit of spending is not to think 'What I need', but 'Can I do without it?'

(a) How does overspending make life difficult?

(b) 'Spending is a part of our life.' Do you agree with the statement? Why/why not?

(c) What difference do you notice between 'I need this' and 'Can I do without this'?

(d) How do advertisements affect our buying habits?

(e) Identify the part of speech of the bold words and write their adjective forms.
solution_html:
(a) Overspending may lead to financial ruin and debt (01 Mark). Moreover, it may create unhappiness within families, cause dissatisfaction and depression. Thus, it makes life difficult (01 Mark).

(b) I agree with the statement (0.5 Marks). In fact, we earn money to spend. We need money to buy foods, clothes and other essentials. We meet the payment for different services and purposes like entertaining people, travelling to different places, helping others and investing in business (1.5 Marks).

(c) "I need this" focuses on desire or impulse, often leading to unnecessary spending (01 Mark). In contrast, "Can I do without this" encourages self-control and helps avoid buying things that are not truly essential (01 Mark).

(d) Advertisements often create a desire for products we don't need by appealing to emotions and making items seem urgently necessary or glamorous (02 Marks).

Instructions:

(i) If the student writes a number instead of another (Ex. “a” instead of “b”), 0.5 marks have to be deducted from each of the numbers.

(ii) Individual marking must be done {Ex. a---0.5, b—0.5, etc.)}.

(iii) Highest marks can be given if there are no mistakes (information, spellings and grammars) ------------08

(e) Here are the parts of speech and the adjective forms of the bold words:

Marks Distribution:

For identifying 04 parts of speech of the bold words (0.25 * 4 = 01 Mark) has to be given and for writing their adjective forms, Students will get (0.25 * 4 = 01 Mark).

question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘The Impact of War on Civilian’ within 10 sentences. 10
solution_html:
The Impact of War on Civilian

War has devastating effects on civilians, who often suffer the most during conflicts. Beyond the battlefield, innocent people face loss of life, injury, displacement, and destruction of homes and infrastructure. Access to basic needs like food, clean water, and healthcare becomes scarce, leading to widespread suffering and long-term health issues. Children are particularly vulnerable, with many losing education opportunities and experiencing psychological trauma. War disrupts communities and economies, leaving lasting scars that hinder development for generations. The impact on civilians highlights the urgent need for global peace and conflict resolution. Promoting dialogue, understanding, and cooperation among nations is essential to prevent violence and protect human rights. Supporting humanitarian efforts and rebuilding war-torn societies can help restore hope and stability. Ultimately, the heavy toll of war on civilians serves as a powerful reminder that lasting peace is vital for humanity’s survival and progress.

Marks Distribution:

Highest Marks ----------------------------------------------09 Marks

(A) For Title (The Impact of War on Civilian) -------------------------------------01 Mark

(B) Topic Sentence (Introduction) -----------------------01 Mark

War has devastating effects on civilians, who often suffer the most during conflicts.

(C) Context Explanation (The Impact of War on Civilian) (Any 6 Points) ----06 Marks
‎• Civilians face loss of life, injury, displacement, and destruction of homes and infrastructure.
‎• Access to basic needs such as food, clean water, and healthcare becomes limited, leading to widespread suffering and long-term health issues.
‎• Children are particularly at risk, losing educational opportunities and suffering psychological trauma.
‎• War disrupts communities and economies, causing lasting damage that hinders development for generations.
‎• The impact on civilians underscores the urgent need for global peace and conflict resolution.
‎• Promoting dialogue, understanding, and cooperation among nations is crucial for preventing violence and protecting human rights.
‎• Supporting humanitarian efforts and rebuilding war-torn societies is essential for restoring hope and stability.

(D) Conclusion -----------------------------------------------------------------01 Mark

In conclusion, the heavy toll of war on civilians serves as a powerful reminder that lasting peace is vital for humanity’s survival and progress.

Instructions:

1. For every 3 spelling/grammatical mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted.

2. We will consider up to 12 sentences. However, if a student writes more than 12 sentences but less than double (20 sentences), 50% of the deserving marks must be deducted. If it becomes double (20 sentences), full marks have to be deducted. Comment: Write within 10 sentences.

3. Students must write within a para. If a student writes more than one para, half of the deserving marks must be deducted. (Comment: Write within single/one para).

4. For writing only the title of the paragraph bears no mark. (comment: Write the Title / The Impact of War on Civilian)

question_html:
Make meaningful sentences with the following words: 10

Impudence, Hesitate, Incorporate, Prodigious, Clinch, Disappear, Acquisition, Discreetly, Contentious, Provide
solution_html:
Impudence (Noun) = Several committee members had the impudence to complain about not being consulted.

Hesitate (Verb) = Should you ever need anything, please don't hesitate to contact me.

Incorporate (Verb) = These conditions must be expressly incorporated into the contract of employment.

Prodigious (Adjective) = The scientist made a prodigious discovery that changed the course of medical research forever.

Clinch (Verb) = The deal was clinched when they agreed to share the shipping costs.

Clinch (Noun) = The final minutes of the debate came down to a heated clinch over the budget proposal.

Disappear (Verb) = He started the treatment and his symptoms disappeared overnight.

Acquisition (Noun) = The acquisition of huge amounts of data has helped our research enormously.

Discreetly (Adverb) = Because of the children involved, they want to resolve the court case as quickly and discreetly as possible.

Contentious (Adjective) = The government’s treatment of refugees remains a highly contentious issue.

Provide (Verb) = The article fails to provide any insight into how these trends are likely to develop.

Instructions:

(i) Any correct and logically meaningful sentence will be accepted. (Sentences with weak meaning will not be allowed.)

(ii) For any grammatical or structural mistake, full marks have to be deducted. Examiners have to show and correct the mistake(s) in the scripts.

(iii) For 02 spelling mistakes, 01 mark will be deducted, for 01 spelling mistake, 0.5 marks will be deducted from that very sentence.
‎(iv) For changing the part of speech of the given word by adding a suffix or prefix, the full marks have to be deducted from the very number. But students can use any form if the given word is a verb (depending on the subject-verb agreement, tense, person, number, etc.)
‎Comment: Use the given word without changing its parts of speech.

question_html:
Add six more sentences to complete the following story: 10

Urmila was a little girl who grew up in a village named Patuair in Kishoreganj district. A small river called Phuleswari flowed through her village. At the age of five, Urmila used to go to the river with her father and started to learn swimming. Her mother did not like the idea of her daughter swimming in the river. But her father was happy to train Urmila to be a competent swimmer. Who knew that the little girl would become an accomplished swimmer one day! …..
solution_html:
Urmila, Story of an Unbeaten Girl.

Urmila was a little girl who grew up in a village named Patuair in Kishoreganj district. A small river called Phuleswari flowed through her village. At the age of five, Urmila used to go to the river with her father and started to learn swimming. Her mother did not like the idea of her daughter swimming in the river. But her father was happy to train Urmila to be a competent swimmer. Who knew that the little girl would become an accomplished swimmer one day! Her passion for swimming intensified day by day. She continued to practice everyday in the river, even during the moonsoon season when the water level rose significantly. The village folks were amazed as Urmila got better and better at swimming. At 14, something big happened-Urmila won a national swimming contest. Everyone was stunned when they saw the scoreboard. Urmila had not only won the competition but had also broken the national record for the event. Her village and parents were really happy. Urmila's journey from the calm Patuair waters to winning nationally showed how strong she was. Her dad's support was a big part of her success. From that day on, Urmila became an inspiration to many young girls in her village who also dreamed of achieving great things.

Marks Distribution:

Highest mark---------------09 Marks

(i) Title (Any logical one) -------(01 Mark has to be deducted for not writing the Title)

“Urmila, Story of an Unbeaten Girl”

(ii) Story (minimum new 6 sentences) ------1.5×6=09 Marks

{If a student adds or writes at least 6-8 new sentences using the given part and completes the story with a proper description without any grammatical or spelling mistakes, he/she will get full marks}.

Instructions:

(i) If a student doesn’t write the given part, 01 mark has to be deducted (comment: write the given part first).

(ii) Students can write the story in any logical way (sample answer isn’t fixed).

(iii) The given part tells us that the story is the incidents of the Past. So, it is normal that the student will develop the story using Past Tense but it can be described in Present Tense and future Tense also by using direct speech.

(iv) If a student doesn’t write the Title, 01 mark has to be deducted (comment: write the Title/ Urmila, Story of an Unbeaten Girl).

(v) If a student writes fewer than five sentences, 01 mark will be deducted for each of the missing sentences. (Comment: Add at least six new sentences).

question_html: Explain the proverb in five sentences: “Grasp all, lose all.” 5
solution_html:
This proverb advises against excessive greed, as it can lead to total loss. A greedy person is never satisfied and often makes poor decisions. Happiness lies in contentment, but greed leads to dissatisfaction. Extreme greed can result in losing everything. The proverb encourages moderation and careful planning to avoid the risks of overreach.

Mark Distribution:

(A) Theme of The Proverb-----------------01 mark
‎• The proverb advises against excessive greed, as it can lead to total loss.

(B) Context of The Proverb (Grasp all, lose all) (03 points) -----03 marks

• Greedy person being unsatisfied, makes wrong decisions.
‎• Emphasizes that greed leads to dissatisfaction.
‎• Extreme greed leads to lose everything.

(C)Conclusion-----------------------------------01 Mark
‎Ultimately, the proverb encourages moderation and careful planning to avoid the risks of overreach.

[N.B. For every 03 spelling or grammatical mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted.]

Instructions:

1. For every 3 spelling/grammatical mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted.

2. We will consider up to 06-07 sentences. However, if a student writes more than 07 sentences, 01 mark has to be deducted from the deserving number.

Comment: Write within five sentences.

question_html:
The sentences in the following text are jumbled. Rearrange in a proper order: 5

(a) Nishat was born on 5 January 1981 in a village called Teori, which is situated in Ramganj upazila in Lakshmipur District.

(b) Though she had a long cherished dream to be a mountaineer, it was not until 2003 that she could climb the 967 feet high Keokradong.

(c) She is also interested in different languages and cultures of the world, and has enrolled for her MA in Japan Studies at DU.

(d) She is the second of four siblings. She is not very tall or well built, or does not have a charming princess look but this apparently ordinary girl has three things that she can be proud of.

(e) The climbing event was organized on 29 May, 2003 to celebrate the 50th Anniversary of Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay's conquest of Mount Everest.

(f) This is an expensive sport, and Nishat and other mountaineers could not have succeeded if several organizations did not help them.

(g) Nishat's life presents an inspirational story that the youth in Bangladesh needs to know.

(h) Nishat joined Bangladesh Mountaineering and Trekking Club in 2006, and completed a basic training course at Darjeeling, India in 2007.

(i) Nishat Mazumder, a Bangladesh woman in her early forties, is a sports icon.

(j) Nishat was an ambassador of “Because I am a Girl” campaign of Plan International’s Bangladesh chapter in her expedition to Mount Everest in 2012.
solution_html:
Instructions:

 If a student writes only a matching/rearranged order, he/she will get full marks. Writing a full passage is not mandatory.

 If the students make a wrong matching, automatically another matching sentence may be wrong.

 If the students use other than a given number format (a, b, c, d…) in the matching row, 01 mark has to be deducted from the deserving marks.

 For every 3 grammatical or spelling mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted. (Depending on the student's matched full sentence writings)

question_html:
Translate the following Bangla paragraph into English: 5

লোকসংগীত গড়ে উঠেছে কোনো একটি নির্দিষ্ট জনগোষ্ঠীর গান ও সুরের সমন্বয়ে, যা কোনো জটিল সাংগীতিক নিয়ম বা কোনো মানসম্মত সংগীতশৈলী দ্বারা প্রভাবিত নয়। বাংলাদেশের রয়েছে লোকসংগীতের এক সমৃদ্ধ ঐতিহ্য, যার মধ্যে ধর্মীয় ও ধর্মনিরপেক্ষ—উভয় ধরনের গানই অন্তর্ভুক্ত। লোকসংগীতকে প্রাচীনকালের সঙ্গীত হিসেবে বর্ণনা করা যেতে পারে, যা একটি জনগোষ্ঠীর হৃদয় থেকে উৎসারিত হয়েছে, তাদের প্রাকৃতিক অভিব্যক্তি প্রকাশের সহজাত প্রক্রিয়ার উপর ভিত্তি করে, যা ধ্রুপদী সঙ্গীত বা আধুনিক জনপ্রিয় গানের নিয়মাবলী দ্বারা প্রভাবিত নয়। সুর, কণ্ঠ এবং বাদ্যযন্ত্র/নৃত্যের সমন্বয়ে সৃষ্ট যেকোনো ধ্বনি বিন্যাসকে সঙ্গীত হিসেবে বর্ণনা করা যেতে পারে। লোকসংগীত হলো গান, সুর এবং নৃত্যের এমন এক সমন্বয় যা সাধারণ মানুষের ঐতিহ্যবাহী সংস্কৃতি থেকে উদ্ভূত হয়েছে।
solution_html:
Translation: Folk music consists of songs and music of a community that are not influenced by any sophisticated musical rules or any standard musical styles. Bangladesh has a heritage of rich folk music which includes both religious and secular songs. Folk music maybe described as the music of the ancient times that sprang from the heart of a community, based on their natural style of expression uninfluenced by the rules of classical music or modern popular songs. Any arrangement of sound created by the combination of tune, voice and instrument/dance may be described as music. Folk music is the combination of song, tune and dance that originate from the traditional culture of ordinary people.

Instructions:
‎* For accurate translation using grammar, punctuation, and sentence structure, students will get full marks. [05 marks for translating 05 sentences correctly]

* For every 03 grammatical/spelling errors 01 mark has to be deducted.

question_html:
Re-write the following text with correct punctuation: 5

He was suddenly impelled to speak.
‎You look a little bit like my mother he said. Especially in the dark by the fire.
‎But you were only four Jerry when you came here. You have remembered how she
‎looked all these years
‎My mother lives in Mannville, he said.
solution_html:
He was suddenly impelled to speak.
‎“You look a little bit like my mother,” he said. “Especially in the dark, by the fire.”
‎“But you were only four, Jerry, when you came here. You have remembered how she
‎looked, all these years?”
‎“My mother lives in Mannville,” he said.

General Instructions for punctuation marks:

(i) Full marks can be given if there are no mistakes / errors in this part.

 For 1 error ---------------01 mark has to be deducted.

 For 05 error or more……………full marks have to be deducted.

(ii) Marks have to be given based on errors not on correct ones. The examiner’s main focus will be on errors not on the correct ones.

(iii) If the student adds or subtracts any word, that every word will be counted as an error and the marks will be deducted according to the above system.

(iv) The examiner has to correct the mistake(s) in the script.

(v) Both “--”/‘--’ formats are correct as quotation marks.

(vi) “--”/‘--’ will be treated as 01 punctuation mark.

question_html:
Fill in each gap of the following passage with appropriate word(s) from the basket given below. Make any grammatical changes (if necessary). 5

In a traditional society like (a)_______, little boys and girls are given separate sets of toys. (b)_________ toys for boys include cars, guns or footballs, girls have to be satisfied with dolls (often Barbie dolls) and doll-houses or miniature cooking utensils. The underlying assumption is that boys are active and full of (c)________, but girls are (d)________ and lack the intelligence or energy to match their male (e)________.
solution_html:
(a) ours

(b) While

(c) vigour

(d) naive

(e) counterparts

Instructions:

Any kind of mistake bears no mark. But mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.

question_html:
Fill in the gaps with suitable words. 5

Art is generally understood as an (a)_______ of human imagination and creative
‎skills in a range of activities (b)_______ painting, drawing, sculpture and architecture. The aim of art is to evoke feelings and emotions that are (c)_______ aesthetic — that is, concerned with beauty— but (d)_______ also can be a form of social commentary. A painting of (e)_______ over the sea glorifies nature, but one that shows a war ravaged town can convey a sense of anguish.
solution_html:
(a) expression

(b) including

(c) considered

(d) art

(e) sunset

Instructions:

Any kind of mistake bears no mark. But mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.

question_html:
Transform the following sentences as directed. 5

(a) Enter the house by this gate. (Passive)

(b) May our women's football team win the SAF championship again. (Assertive)

(c) No sooner had I left the place than he went away. (Affirmative)

(d) I like to read books. (Complex)

(e) You should never tell a lie. (Imperative)
solution_html:
(a) You are requested to enter the house by this gate.

(b) I wish our women's football team could win the SAF championship again.

(c) As soon as I left the place he went away.

(d) It is the act of reading books, which I like.

(e) Never tell a lie.

Instructions:

(i) For any kind of grammatical mistake (i.e. subject, verb, tense etc.), full marks have to be deducted.

(ii) For missing Question Mark /Exclamation Mark in sentence 0.5 marks have to be deducted from each deserving marks.

(iii) In case of spelling mistakes,

* For 1/2 errors----0.5 marks have to be deducted.

* For 03 errors------01 mark has to be deducted.

N.B. Alternative answers can be accepted.

question_html: Write the theme of the poem ‘The Schoolboy’ by William Blake within 5 sentences. 5
solution_html:
The theme of the poem is the contrast between the freedom and joy of nature and the oppressive, joyless experience of a child forced into schooling. It expresses the speaker's sorrow over the loss of innocence and the natural happiness of youth, symbolized by the bird and the child, constrained by societal expectations. The poem critiques the impact of education and societal pressures on the youthful spirit, urging reflection on the value of nurturing happiness and freedom in children.

Marks Distribution:

Highest Mark--------------------------------------05 marks

(A) The Theme of the Poem ---------01 Mark • The poem illustrates the contrast between the freedom and joy of nature and the oppressive, joyless experience of a child forced into schooling.

(B) Context Explanation (The Schoolboy) (Any 4 Points) ---------(01 × 04) = 04 Marks
‎• It expresses sorrow over the loss of innocence and natural happiness.
‎• The bird and child symbolize the constrained youthful spirit, affected by societal expectations.
‎• It critiques the impact of education and societal pressures on children.
‎• The poem urges reflection on the importance of nurturing happiness and freedom in youth.

Instructions:

1. For every 3 spelling/grammatical mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted.

2. We will consider up to 06-07 sentences. However, if a student writes more than 07 sentences, 01 mark has to be deducted from the deserving number.

Comment: Write within five sentences.
